methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Chariot Defense is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Adam Warmoth that has raised $41M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set comprising 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities in the defense sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $41M total raised as a proxy baseline, implying a $82M valuation. Peer-median analysis references 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $225M, ranging from $125M (NODA AI) to $500M (Stark Defence). The median peer valuation reflects the upper bound of capital-intensity and risk profile typical for Series A defense companies.

The triangulated analysis produces a low estimate of $70M, a median of $154M, and a high of $259M. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by the $143M gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods—reflects uncertainty around how much of the $41M raised translates to demonstrated enterprise value versus burn. Key variables affecting final valuation include revenue traction relative to peers, customer concentration, and regulatory approval status. The peer range of $125M–$500M suggests substantial variance in how the market prices Series A defense-sector risk and market opportunity.
